title: Debt, rent and other cash collectors
description: Organize activities to raise funds or otherwise solicit and gather monetary donations or other gifts for an organization. May design and produce promotional materials. May also raise awareness of the organization's work, goals, and financial needs. Assist judges in court or by conducting research or preparing legal documents. Assist lawyers by investigating facts, preparing legal documents, or researching legal precedent. Conduct research to support a legal proceeding, to formulate a defense, or to initiate legal action. (Based on 20 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Record transaction information on forms or logs, and notify designated personnel of discrepancies. Keep records of merchandise distributed and money collected. Maintain records of machine maintenance and repair. Sort and file correspondence and perform miscellaneous clerical duties, such as answering correspondence and writing reports. Monitor budgets, expense reports, or other financial data for fundraising organizations. Keep accurate records of monetary exchanges, authorization forms, and transaction reconciliations. Record information about financial status of customers and status of collection efforts. Develop or implement fundraising activities, such as annual giving campaigns or direct mail programs. Locate and monitor overdue accounts, using computers and a variety of automated systems.

Answer customers' questions about services and charges, or direct them to customer service centers. Assist customers by providing information and resolving their complaints. Explain customer inquiries or explain cost, availability, policies, and procedures of facilities. Answer customer questions regarding problems with their accounts. Greet customers entering establishments. Provide customer assistance and information, such as giving directions or handling wheelchairs. Provide assistance with patrons' special needs, such as helping those with wheelchairs. Explain and calculate parking charges, collect fees from customers, and respond to customer complaints.

Persuade customers to pay amounts due on credit accounts, damage claims, or nonpayable checks, or to return merchandise. Contact corporate representatives, government officials, or community leaders to increase awareness of organizational causes, activities, or needs. Advise customers of necessary actions and strategies for debt repayment. Confer with customers by telephone or in person to determine reasons for overdue payments and to review the terms of sales, service, or credit contracts.

Notify credit departments, order merchandise repossession or service disconnection, and turn over account records to attorneys when customers fail to respond to collection attempts. Trace delinquent customers to new addresses by inquiring at post offices, telephone companies, credit bureaus, or through the questioning of neighbors. Locate and notify customers of delinquent accounts by mail, telephone, or personal visits to solicit payment. Arrange for debt repayment or establish repayment schedules, based on customers' financial situations. Negotiate credit extensions when necessary.

Secure commitments of participation or donation from individuals or corporate donors. Solicit cash or in-kind donations or sponsorships from individual, business, or government donors. Identify and build relationships with potential donors. Conduct research to identify the goals, net worth, charitable donation history, or other data related to potential donors, potential investors, or general donor markets. Write reports or prepare presentations to communicate fundraising program data. Create or update donor databases.

Monitor progress of fundraising drives. Direct or supervise fundraising staff, including volunteer staff members. Plan and direct special events for fundraising, such as silent auctions, dances, golf events, or walks. Develop fundraising activity plans that maximize participation or contributions and minimize costs. Develop strategies to encourage new or increased contributions. Attend community events, meetings, or conferences to promote organizational goals or solicit donations or sponsorships.

Design or produce materials such as posters, Web sites, or newsletters to promote, market, or advertise fundraising events. Write speeches, press releases, or other promotional materials to increase awareness of the causes, missions, or goals of organizations seeking funds. Secure speakers for charitable events, community meetings, or conferences to increase awareness of charitable, nonprofit, or political causes. Prepare materials such as fundraising envelopes, bid sheets, or gift bags for charitable events. Compile or develop materials to submit to granting or other funding organizations.

Compile and maintain non-monetary reports and records. Calculate total payments received during a time period, and reconcile this with total sales. Keep periodic balance sheets of amounts and numbers of transactions. Install machines, making the necessary water and electrical connections in compliance with codes. Disassemble and assemble machines, according to specifications and using hand and power tools.

Remove relevant tasks as they do not apply to the occupation of Debt, rent and other cash collectors based on the merging process. Adjust machine pressure gauges and thermostats, perform minor repairs on machines, assist with duties in other areas of the store, and operate machines unrelated to cash collection and fundraising.

Record transaction information, locate and notify customers about overdue accounts, negotiate repayment plans, and track payment status using various systems. Answer inquiries, provide assistance to customers, and monitor compliance with fundraising goals. Manage promotional materials and marketing strategies to maximize donor participation.
Provide assistance to the public, such as directions to court offices and information about facilities, entertainment options, and rules and regulations.
Prepare, edit, or review legal documents, including legislation, briefs, pleadings, appeals, wills, contracts, and real estate closing statements.
Gather and analyze research data, such as statutes, decisions, and legal articles, codes, and documents to support legal proceedings.
Investigate facts and law of cases and search pertinent sources to determine causes of action and prepare cases.
Conduct personal background investigations or search for evidence for cases such as divorce, custody, or missing persons.
Obtain and analyze information on suspects, crimes, or disturbances to solve cases and gather evidence for court.
Testify at hearings or court trials to present evidence and support case arguments.
Monitor operations to ensure tax and legal regulations are adhered to, and report all violations or suspicious behaviors.
Arrest persons in court when arrest warrants have been issued and provide security for courtroom proceedings.
Examine documentation to verify ownership, restrictions, and legal descriptions of properties as part of title investigations.
Supervise or train personnel, such as law students or volunteers, related to legal proceedings or court management.
Record details of attendance, sales, receipts, or activities related to court or event proceedings.
Review files, complaints, and court documents to determine issues involved in cases being heard.
Develop and maintain a log of observations and surveillance, ensuring adherence to regulations.
Gather and manage evidence, including controlling exhibits and maintaining order during court trials.
Prepare affidavits and maintain organized filing systems for legal documents in physical or electronic formats.
Keep abreast of changes in laws and inform judges of how cases may be affected by these changes.
